3.happening: Set the left boundary at index 0.
3.why: The search window starts covering the whole array.
3.invariant: The minimum is always within [left, right].

4.happening: Set the right boundary at the last index ({right}).
4.why: The window's right end starts at the array's end.
4.invariant: The minimum is always within [left, right].

5.happening: The window still spans more than one element — keep narrowing.
5.why: We halve the range until a single element remains.
5.invariant: The minimum is always within [left, right].

5.happening: left has met right — the window is a single element.
5.why: The search space collapsed to the answer.

6.happening: Compute the midpoint mid = {mid} of the window.
6.why: We probe the middle to decide which half to keep.
6.invariant: mid is between left and right.

7.happening: nums[mid] = {nums[mid]} > nums[right] = {nums[right]} — the pivot is to the right of mid.
7.why: A middle larger than the right end means the drop-off (minimum) lies further right.

7.happening: nums[mid] = {nums[mid]} <= nums[right] = {nums[right]} — mid's half is sorted down to it.
7.why: The minimum is at mid or to its left.

8.happening: Discard mid and the left half — move left to mid + 1.
8.why: The minimum lies strictly to the right of mid.
8.invariant: The minimum is still within [left, right].

10.happening: Discard the right half — pull right in to mid.
10.why: The minimum is at mid or to its left, so mid stays a candidate.
10.invariant: The minimum is still within [left, right].

11.happening: left and right have met — read the minimum at nums[left] = {nums[left]}.
11.why: The window collapsed to exactly the smallest element.

init.happening: Placing left and right at the two ends of the array.
init.why: The whole array is the initial search window.

loop.happening: Checking whether the window still spans more than one element.
loop.why: We halve until a single element remains.

update.happening: Computing the midpoint of the current window.
update.why: The midpoint is the value we probe.
update.invariant: mid is between left and right.

check.happening: Comparing nums[mid] with nums[right] to locate the pivot side.
check.why: This tells us which half to discard.
check.invariant: One side of mid is always sorted.

move.happening: Narrowing the window — discarding the half that can't hold the minimum.
move.why: Each step halves the search space (O(log n)).

return.happening: Returning the minimum once the window collapsed.
return.why: left == right pinpoints the smallest element.
